calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Set For Trial On Jun-22-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-17-2020 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Howard Memorial Hospital
Off Calendar May-22-2020 Off Calendar

Defendant Howard Memorial Hospitals Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue (Ccp 397(C))
Off Calendar May-21-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HOWARD MEMORIAL HOSPITAL DBA ADVENTIST HEALTH's Motion For Change Of Venue (Ccp 397(C))

Defendant Howard Memorial Hospital's motion to change venue under Code Civ. Proc. 397(c) is granted. Section 397(c) is discretionary and permits transfer from one proper county to another proper county when "the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The burden is on the party seeking a change of venue to prove that both the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change. (Corfee v. Southern California Edison Co. (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) In this professional negligence action, Plaintiff alleges that she had back surgery performed in Mendocino County, and that her pre-surgical, surgical and post-surgical care was below the standard of care, resulting in severe personal injury. Defendant shows based on Plaintiff's deposition testimony and discovery responses that the vast majority of third-party witnesses, including Plaintiff's current treating healthcare providers and others, reside or work in Mendocino County. (See Allen Decl. 3-6 & Exs. B-E.) Defendant's showing, including the reasonable inferences from these facts, meets its burden to show that the convenience of witnesses would be promoted by a change in venue from San Francisco County to Mendocino County. Plaintiff's claim that Defendant unreasonably delayed in filing its motion lacks merit.

Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
